godFlavour.Paladine: Law, protection, justice — the high god who watches over all who serve the light.
godFlavour.Kiri-Jolith: Battle, courage, honour — the god who steadies soldiers before the fight.
godFlavour.Mishakal: Healing, mercy, compassion — the god who mends what is broken.
godFlavour.Habbakuk: Nature, perseverance, the living world — the god who guards what grows.
godFlavour.Branchala: Music, joy, inspiration — the god who believes the world can be beautiful.

choiceFlags.comment: Fighter choosing: orthodox keeper of the Measure
Fighter choosing: reformer of the Measure
Fighter reformer sends letter to High Council
Stayed to help the burning village survivors

choiceFilters.comment: Only show if player chose to report in Scene 44
Only show if player chose to move refugees in Scene 44
Only show if player chose to defend in Scene 44
Only show if player chose to find Caeron in Scene 44
Scene 8: class-split — Fighters cannot pick C or D
Dispatch pouch leverage requires having kept it
Dispatch pouch reference check — disabled but visible if missing
Requires Scene 53B choice B or C

hubCompanionSwaps.swapText: A correspondent at the east gate is asking sharp questions.
Visit the infirmary. There are refugees who need help.

mandatoryDetours.label: Vael has news about their mission.

26.text: The draconians carry nothing of value. Their gear crumbles with their bodies.
A few steel pieces in a belt pouch.
Steel pieces and a sealed dispatch pouch.
The patrol leader carries a masterwork shortsword.

43.text: The wounded draconian carries nothing but a broken blade.
A patrol sketch tucked into its belt.
The sketch and a small purse of coins.

51.text: A few coins and crossbow bolts.
The corporal's purse and a signal whistle.

65.text: The Sivak's body shifts and changes. Unsettling.
Sealed orders naming you specifically — chilling.
The Sivak officer carried a fine blade and sealed orders.

70.text: Coins and a well-made signal whistle.
The sergeant carried a finely balanced blade.

11_PATROL.text: Nothing useful — broken arrows, spoiled rations.
Steel pieces and a crude poison vial.
A stash of coins and a well-made dagger.
Coins, a dagger, and a crude map of the garrison perimeter.
A finely balanced dagger with a serrated edge — not goblin work.

44C.text: Rations and broken equipment. Nothing useful.
A few coins from the soldiers.
Coins and a Kapak venom vial — handle with care.
The supply cache yields coins, bandages, and a potion.
A significant stash — coins, potions, and a finely crafted crossbow.
Among the supplies: a vial of antitoxin and a gleaming shortsword.

28_CARAVAN.text: Coins and a potion tucked in a belt.
The deserter carried considerable coin and a fine blade.

53_BREACH.text: The draconians turned to stone. There is nothing to search.
One draconian's belt pouch survived the transformation.
A pouch with coins and a crude Dragon Army officer's signet.

53_SABOTAGE.text: Oil-soaked rags and fire-starting tools. Nothing useful.
Coins and a Dragon Army signet ring — intelligence value.
A significant coin purse and coded orders.

64E.text: Standard soldier equipment, burned at the edges.
A few coins from the soldiers.
The Bozak carried spell components and coin.
An officer's satchel with patrol routes and supply maps.
Among the Bozak's belongings: a wand that hums with residual energy.

70A.text: The scout's journal — attack timing and troop positions.
Coins, the journal, and a Dragon Army attack route map.
Everything above, plus the scout's enchanted spyglass.

70C_SCOUTS.text: Coins and a partial battle plan for Vogler.
The full battle plan — troop movements, assault timing, everything.

ROAD_OA2_FIGHT.text: Drask's men carried little of value. Rough weapons, rougher coin.
A handful of steel pieces from the thugs' pockets.
Drask's heavy axe is well-maintained. Worth something.
Drask's personal stash — hidden under the tavern floor.

MIRA.*TRAVEL BUNDLE.historyNote: Mira’s bundle: dried yarrow, 2 clean bandages, pine salve.

questObjectives.name: March to Vogler
questObjectives.desc: Escort the refugee column south to the village of Vogler

questObjectives.name: Dispatch Pouch
questObjectives.desc: Deliver the sealed dispatch to Commander Serath

questObjectives.name: Earn Your Knighthood
questObjectives.desc: Prove yourself worthy of the Order through deeds

questObjectives.name: Aldric's Trust
questObjectives.desc: Earn the respect of your fellow squire

questObjectives.name: Defend Vogler
questObjectives.desc: Protect the village from the Dragon Army
